title: Congress demands action against former Home Secretary over 1993 firing incident
content: The party has also vowed to strengthen their fight against the BJP and bring a change in the central power in 2029.

title: Outbound portability of medical scheme benefits Rajasthan’s patients
content: Over 5,900 claims amounting to ₹17 crore have been submitted by the patients for speciality treatment in States such as Gujarat, Haryana, Maharashtra and Delhi. These treatments included heart stent procedures, heart bypass surgery, cancer surgery, chemotherapy, dialysis and Caesarean deliveries

title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana border en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers wanted to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped them by erecting barricades, Sarwan Singh Pandher, farmer leader and coordinator of the Desh Bachao Morcha, says

title: Former Odisha CM Naveen Patnaik demands resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Terming the situation “very serious” and “very grave”. he said a number of students were violently attacked and injured

title: Uttar Pradesh cabinet approves free scooty scheme for college girls
content: The scheme will play a significant role in ensuring easier access to higher education for girl students while making them self-reliant and confident, said the State government. Eligible female students will be selected based on merit from the list comprising the top 5% of female graduates

title: Clean energy push is India’s answer to transition challenges: UN climate chief
content: Simon Stiell, Executive Secretary of UN Climate Change, praised India as a “solar superpower”, noting that fossil-fuel-free sources now make up half of its installed power capacity

title: Russian envoy summoned over death of Indian crew
content: The Ministry of External Affairs said that it has conveyed “India’s grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation” over the attack to the Russia envoy

title: Thousands brave rain and regroup at CJP protest site, day after police crackdown
content: From only about 200-300 people early in the morning, crowd swells to thousands by evening; many first-timers say ‘brutal’ police action made them join the protest site

title: NIA court allows special dietary arrangements for U.S. national accused in a terror case
content: Preserving the accused’s dignity requires allowing him access to food he is accustomed to eating, and meals prepared according to his dietary requirements, the defence argues

title: Study to evaluate impact of heat stress on health in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka
content: Participants will receive a digital watch to keep track of body temperature;  1,200 households roped in per site for the study; project to be taken up at Perungudi and Poompuhar in Tamil Nadu, and Sirwar and Chalakarre in Karnataka

title: Gold ornaments, bronze objects stolen from temple
content: The police said that the isolated location of the temple and lack of security measures including CCTV cameras in the temple could have led to the theft

title: BJP condemns Speaker’s observations on DMK MLA arrest
content: BJP State president Nainar Nagenthran said that it was not only a tradition, but also part of rules that the Speaker was to be informed if an MP or an MLA was arrested

title: Around 1.75 tonnes of red sanders stolen from sandalwood depot
content: Forest officials said that based on a complaint by Jothi Lingam who was in charge of the depot, a case was registered by Tirupattur Town police

title: Two persons held for feeding gutka to baby
content: A video of the incident, including that of the child being fed gutka surfaced on social media, inviting public outcry

title: Storage in Karnataka’s Cauvery reservoirs stands at 52% of total capacity as CWMA is set to review situation on July 22
content: The Cauvery Water Management Authority, which is the apex body that monitors implementation of Cauvery water release to lower riparian States as per the Supreme Court’s verdict, is meeting in Delhi to review the situation

title: Applications invited for trustees in more temples
content: This time, apart from Aadhar cards and address proof, applicants have been asked to submit a no-objection certificate from the respective temple and a verification certificate from the police

title: Karnataka High Court seeks State’s response on plea seeking CBI probe into ‘illegalities’ in KPSC recruitments
content: The High Court of Karnataka has directed the State government to file its response on a petition seeking a probe by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) into the allegations of illegalities in the Karnataka State Public Service Commission recruitments to posts of Group A and B Gazetted Probationers and allegations against Shivashankar S. Sahukar, suspended chairperson.

title: Kerala eyes major GCC investments with new strategic roadmap
content: Minister Kunhalikutty said the report is a vital step for transforming Kerala into the most preferred GCC destination in the country

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: Toxic gas kills 12 in Sikkim hydropower tunnel
content: According to the NHPC, 25 workers were inside the under-construction tunnel of the Teesta State-VI Hydroelectric Project in Namchi district

title: IIT Roorkee asks students, staff to refrain from political activity without prior approval
content: The advisory comes after the institute says it had noticed on social media platforms posts by some members of campus community expressing support for or criticism of ongoing political movements

title: Unannounced midnight detention of train upsets pilgrims’ travel plans to Guruvayur
content: Passengers says travelling in the new Jan Shatabdi coach seats, which have little or no reclining angle, for four to six hours often results in back pain and a harrowing experience. The earlier trains had reclining seats with armrests.

title: Vazhipadu materials to be procured and issued exclusively by Central Store, TDB tells Kerala HC
content: HC satisfied with TDB decision to discontinue practice of administrative officer acting as supplier for Vazhipadu materials in Sabarimala

title: CFTRI, NABARD join hands to bolster agro-food processing in Kalyana Karnataka
content: The collaboration aims to support the Farmer Training and Common Facility Centres established by NABARD across seven districts, namely Bidar, Yadgir, Kalaburagi, Raichur, Koppal, Ballari and Vijayanagara.

title: Kin of seafarers killed in Hormuz attacks demand UN intervention, compensation, govt. jobs
content: Forward Seamen’s Union of India general secretary Manoj Yadav says Union Government has not raised a strong enough voice against these killings or ensured justice to bereaved families

title: Tamil rapper Arivu detained during bid to protest against NEET near Secretariat in Chennai, CM Vijay meets him
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay invited Arivu — who is best known for his Tamil independent music including the hit song Enjoy Enjaami, Thalapathy Kacheri from ‘Jana Nayagan’ — to his chamber and listened to the singer’s concerns.

title: Tribunal sets aside most of the FEMA violation charges against Lalit Modi, BCCI
content: The Appellate Tribunal under the Smugglers and Foreign Exchange Manipulators (Forfeiture of Property) Act has largely overturned the ED’s conclusions. However, it upheld the ED’s findings on two specific counts

title: Union Education Minister Pradhan says Rahul, Congress exploiting students; CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site draws Opposition leaders | LIVE
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Medanta Hospital

title: Pawar, Kejriwal, other Opposition leaders visit Jantar Mantar after crackdown; back CJP protest
content: Sharad Pawar, accompanied by party MP Supriya Sule, met injured protesters and members of the movement and condemned the police action during Monday's (July 20, 2026) attempted march to Parliament.

title: Rahul changed goalposts to end sit-in, says govt.; BJP terms protest at PM’s residence a ‘symbol of anarchy’
content: Union Minister Jitendra Singh, the government’s interlocutor, says the Congress leader initially agreed to suspend his protest if the paper leak issue was discussed in Parliament, but he later raised the demand for Pradhan’s ouster; Pradhan says Rahul exploiting students as a ‘political tool’

title: SIT formed to look into allegations against Art of Living, says Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister
content: The Karnataka government has constituted a 11-member Special Investigation Team led by Bengaluru Regional Commissioner Amlan Aditya Biswas to probe the alleged grabbing of government land by the Art of Living ashram and its affiliate organisations on the outskirts of Bengaluru

title: Congress takes out Lok Bhavan march in Thiruvananthapuram
content: The police bid to round up some protesters leads to heated exchanges. The protesters shout slogans against the police and in support of the Youth Congress

title: SC sets aside death sentence in 1996 Rajasthan bus blast case, orders fresh trial
content: The proceedings stand vitiated by the denial of effective legal representation and other procedural lapses that undermined the constitutional guarantee of a fair trial, the apex court says

title: Rahul Gandhi, Congress MPs detained after taking paper leak protest to the PM’s doorstep
content: Congress MPs march 100 metres from Congress chief’s house to the PM’s residence, raising four demands; forcibly cleared by Delhi police after three hours; “beginning of the end” for Modi regime, says Congress leader

title: Madhya Pradesh Assembly passes UCC Bill amid Congress sloganeering
content: The Bill seeks to criminalises polygamy and nikaha halala, while also making registration of live-in relationships mandatory

title: Pedestrians welcome Shivajinagar footpath clearance despite attack
content: The Street Vendors’ Joint Action Committee condemned the attack on the officials and said it was a deliberate attempt by some people to link it with street vendors

title: Court rejects bail pleas of seven accused in attack on ED team
content: The court dismissed the bail pleas filed by Sreejith, Dineeth, Amal, Shyju Salim, Anil Kumar, Nishad and Dinakar

title: Rain soaks Hyderabad on July 20-21; several Telangana districts receive intense showers
content: The India Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm forecast for 11 Telangana districts for Wednesday (July 22)

title: KMRL urged to repair Kochi metro diversion roads, ease bottlenecks
content: People’s representatives cite potholes, damaged road shoulders and worsening congestion on key diversion routes; residents seek widening of Vennala High School Junction and removal of encroachments from footpaths; metro agency says it has sought ₹7.5 crore from the State government to repair diversion roads and streamline traffic

title: Farmers breach Baldota factory premises during protest in Koppal
content: A protest organised as part of the 46th Farmers’ Martyrs’ Day turned tense near the Baldota factory on the Koppal-Bengaluru National Highway on Tuesday after hundreds of farmers breached the factory premises by breaking sections of the compound wall and damaging the main gate

title: 'Govt had no interest in debate': Rahul on why Cong protested outside PM's residence
content: Rahul Gandhi addressed students and the Prime Minister concerning the NEET paper leak.  He demanded a proper discussion in Lok Sabha about the examination system.  The Congress party protested outside the Prime Minister's residence over the issue.  Gandhi questioned the collapsing Indian education system and rising exam costs.  He asserted the opposition stands with students for reforms and justice.

title: Were Argentina under more pressure than usual? Messi's pep talks fuel theories – Watch
content: Fresh footage shows Lionel Messi urging Argentina to forget everything before the World Cup final.  The team delivered a subdued performance and ultimately lost the match to Spain.  Messi's repeated message has fueled speculation among fans and observers.  Coach Lionel Scaloni also hinted at pre-match issues without elaboration.  The viral videos continue to deepen the mystery surrounding the team's mindset.

title: 'Open to both genders': 5,500 apply for Ram Temple Trust CEO post
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is seeking a new Chief Executive Officer. Over five thousand five hundred applications have been received for the position. A selection committee is currently screening candidates based on experience and qualifications. The appointment of the new CEO is expected around mid-August. The Trust will also fill three vacant trustee positions soon.

title: Europe’s biggest tech firm is offering employees $20K-plus bonus if they stay until 2030
content: ASML is reportedly offering employees a significant retention bonus. Eligible employees worldwide will receive a conditional share award valued at twenty thousand euros. This award will be granted on January 1, 2027, and will vest on January 1, 2030. The company is experiencing continued growth and development, investing in innovation and expansion. This move contrasts with US companies which have seen substantial layoffs this year.

title: France passes law banning social media for children under 15
content: France has approved a nationwide ban on social media for children under fifteen. This new law also prohibits mobile phone use within high schools across the nation. President Macron wants the ban to begin at the start of the next school year. This measure aims to protect young users from harmful online content and its effects. Several other European countries are also considering similar restrictions on digital platforms.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: Bill Koch’s Aspen estate once asked $125m, now poised to sell for $33.5m
content: Billionaire Bill Koch's expansive Aspen estate has sold for $33.515 million after a luxury auction.  The property, initially listed at $125 million, struggled to find a buyer for two years.  After several price reductions, the 52-acre Elk Mountain Lodge went to a competitive bidding process.  This sale highlights shifts in the ultra-luxury housing market and buyer selectivity.  The transaction represents a notable luxury home sale in Aspen this year.

title: Aquarium fish escapes into crater lake, scientists fear biodiversity risk
content: Flowerhorn cichlids, bred by humans, have found their way into Lake Sampaloc, a crater lake in the Philippines. Their presence poses a significant threat to the local ecosystem, competing aggressively with native ayungin fish and disrupting aquaculture. Experts raise alarms about the potential for disease transmission and the rapid expansion of this invasive species, underscoring a pressing global issue of domesticated animals affecting natural habitats.

title: US mosquito spraying backfires as insects evolve resistance across 84% blocks
content: New research indicates mosquito spraying is causing insect resistance.  Genetic markers for resistance are now widespread across many residential blocks.  This resistance is more prevalent in wealthier neighborhoods where spraying occurs frequently.  Overuse of chemicals is making insecticides less effective over time.  Integrated pest management offers a more sustainable solution for mosquito control.

title: US airlines to resume direct flights to Lebanon after 40 years, says Donald Trump
content: US President Donald Trump announced direct flights to Lebanon will resume. This decision follows talks with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun at the White House. Direct commercial flights were suspended between the two nations in 1985. The US pledged continued support for Lebanon and its stability. Airlines may be cautious due to ongoing regional conflicts and security concerns.

title: Health minister JP Nadda, MoS Jitendra Singh meet Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital
content: Union ministers made a notable visit to activist Sonam Wangchuk, currently hospitalized at Medanta in Gurugram. This follows the Delhi High Court's ruling, which facilitated his urgent transfer from Safdarjung Hospital after his wife's appeal contested a previous decision. As Wangchuk enters the 24th day of his hunger strike, protests continue to erupt at Jantar Mantar over the alleged NEET paper leak.

title: WWE RAW: Solo Sikoa offers Seth Rollins alliance as Bloodline dominates
content: The July 20 WWE Monday Night RAW event in Detroit featured several exciting tag team and singles matches. Bron Breakker and Austin Theory secured a victory in the opening tag team bout. Oba Femi defeated JD McDonagh and challenged Brock Lesnar to a future match. LA Knight faced Jacob Fatu in the main event, with Fatu emerging victorious.

title: Lamine Yamal's girlfriend Inés García sparks debate with latest update
content: Lamine Yamal has been in a high profile relationship with popular social media influencer, Inés García Santos, for the last few months. As Spain won the 2026 World Cup, their relationship has sparked outrage among fans.

title: Dylan Larkin likely to stay with Red Wings despite trade speculation
content: Trade speculation about Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin remains. But recent developments suggest a deal may not be imminent. Negotiations have been complicated by changes in the leadership of the organization and reported trade demands, but insiders think the franchise could keep Larkin through the regular season. The uncertainty also raises new questions about Detroit’s captaincy and long-term roster plans.

title: Can Pithoragarh’s bn-yr-old fossils help it become India’s first Unesco Global Geopark?
content: Pithoragarh district in Uttarakhand has revealed ancient stromatolite fossil colonies. These fossils represent some of Earth's earliest known evidence of life. Researchers found similar geological formations in nearby areas, suggesting regional significance. UNESCO Global Geoparks recognize significant geological sites for conservation and development. India currently lacks a UNESCO Global Geopark, with Pithoragarh as a strong contender.

title: Ravens, Commanders eye $90m former WR in NFL free agency
content: Stefon Digs, the 32 year old wide receiver, has performed well on the field in the last season but he was released by the New England Patriots. The Baltimore Ravens and the Washington Commanders might be eyeing himnow.

title: 'We owe students more than outrage': Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
content: Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused Rahul Gandhi and Congress of exploiting students politically.  He stated they staged a protest outside the Prime Minister's residence, causing public inconvenience.  The minister added that Congress chose spectacle over democratic debate and solutions for students.  He emphasized the government's commitment to discussing NEET and addressing youth concerns.  The government aims to provide answers, reforms, and accountability to students.

title: The sudden dharna: How Congress surprised its own MPs, govt with protest outside PM's residence
content: Congress leaders staged a protest at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence. They demanded the resignation of the Prime Minister and education minister. Many party leaders were unaware of the protest plan beforehand. Police forcibly removed Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi from the site. The government sent officials to persuade the opposition leaders to leave.

title: 'If someone dies .... ': Police probes alleged chats ahead of CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march
content: Delhi Police are examining alleged chat messages from the Cockroach Janta Party's protest march.  These conversations reportedly discussed maintaining communication during potential internet shutdowns.  Some messages also referred to participants being prepared to sacrifice their lives for the cause.  Investigators are verifying the authenticity and origin of these digital communications.  Multiple FIRs have been registered as the probe into the violent clashes continues.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for July 22
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 22, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: John Dewey’s quote on learning: Why children learn best through meaningful experiences
content: John Dewey championed active and meaningful education, connecting classroom learning to real life experiences. His philosophy transformed teaching methods, moving away from rote memorization towards hands-on engagement. Dewey's ideas remain relevant today, influencing modern educational practices and professional development programs. He stressed that education should prepare students for meaningful participation in society. This approach helps individuals understand and navigate life more effectively.

title: Cale Makar's wife Tracy Evans: Inside their love story and marriage
content: Cale Makar and wife Tracy Evans have one of hockey's most enduring love stories, dating back to their middle school years in Calgary. Makar is still the king of the NHL; Evans has had a successful teaching career, out of the limelight. Here’s what we know about Tracy Evans, their relationship, engagement, wedding and life together.

title: Training gear, ceremony kits missing: Indian boxers hit by luggage chaos at CWG 2026
content: India's boxing contingent faces luggage delays before the 2026 Commonwealth Games. Several boxers and coaches arrived in Glasgow without essential training equipment and attire. This marks the second baggage issue encountered during their recent travel. Despite these challenges, the team aims for a strong medal performance. Indian boxing legend MC Mary Kom expressed confidence in the team's medal potential.

title: Kaylee Hottle, deaf Godzilla vs. Kong star, dies at 18 in tragic car crash
content: Kaylee Hottle, the deaf actor best known for playing Jia in Godzilla vs Kong and Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire, has died. She was 18. Hottle died following a car accident in Frederick, Maryland, on Tuesday, July 21. Her father, Joshua Hottle, announced the news during a Facebook livestream in American Sign Language.

title: Canada joins next-generation fighter jet programme led by UK, Italy and Japan
content: Canada has joined the Global Combat Air Programme as an observer nation. This move allows privileged access to the UK, Italy, and Japan-led initiative. The program aims to develop a sixth-generation stealth fighter jet by 2035. Canada's participation diversifies its defense partnerships beyond the United States. This inclusion marks the program's first expansion since its 2022 launch.

title: Watch: Lionel Messi returns home to Rosario; fans wait hours for a glimpse
content: Lionel Messi returned to his hometown of Rosario for rest after Argentina's World Cup final defeat. Hundreds of supporters gathered early hoping to see their captain, but he did not make a public appearance. Messi's SUV was glimpsed as it departed the airport for his family's residence. He also plans to reunite with his father who was undergoing medical treatment.

title: India's growth remains strong, but trade barriers threaten 2047 goal: WTO
content: India's strong economic growth continues, but structural reforms are needed for developed nation status. High trade costs and regulatory complexity present significant challenges to global integration. The World Trade Organization report notes progress in trade facilitation and financial inclusion. India is actively pursuing free trade agreements to boost its export strategy. Achieving a larger share of global exports requires balancing self-reliance with greater openness.

title: 'World Cup lost its credibility': Blatter says politics overshadowed football
content: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter criticised the 2026 World Cup's credibility. He urged fans and associations to reclaim football from political influence. Spain defeated Argentina to win the expanded 48-team tournament. FIFA president Gianni Infantino highlighted record attendance and viewership figures. Despite controversies, the tournament saw strong on-field performances and high viewership.

title: Donald Trump's tariff tornado returns as India deal stalls
content: Donald Trump plans to impose new tariffs on many countries, including India. These tariffs will affect global trade and financial markets significantly. India's trade deal negotiations with the US face potential complications. The US administration cites unfair trade practices and forced labor concerns. This move escalates trade tensions and impacts international commerce.

title: 'How to kill, dispose bodies': Bengaluru triple murder accused turned to AI, say cops
content: Bengaluru police are delving into a disturbing triple murder involving an AI chatbot. Reports reveal that the prime suspect meticulously plotted the murders over six months, using AI to seek counsel on execution tactics, body disposal techniques, and cleaning traces of the crime. Financial issues and family discord appear to have driven the heinous act. The couple involved was captured after attempting to flee to Puducherry.

title: 'It's embarrassing': Argentina's World Cup final conduct under fresh scrutiny
content: New footage shows an alleged attempt to influence the referee after Enzo Fernandez's red card.  Argentina full-back Nicolas Tagliafico appeared to feign injury moments after the foul occurred.  Lionel Messi then rushed towards the referee, pointing to the fallen Tagliafico.  This effort did not change the referee's decision regarding Fernandez's dismissal.  The match concluded with Spain winning their second World Cup title.

title: India buys more Russian, Latin American crude as Middle East supplies dip
content: India stepped up imports from Moscow once Washington granted a temporary waiver, helping offset reduced supplies from Gulf producers caused by the disruption of traffic through the strategically important Strait of Hormuz.

title: Aamir Khan’s half-brother struggles with schizophrenia and homelessness, claims ex-wife Eva Grover
content: Actress Eva Grover revealed her past marriage to Hyder Ali Khan involved domestic violence. She stated his schizophrenia and alcohol use worsened their relationship significantly. Grover endured abuse for five years before initiating divorce proceedings. She was separated from her daughter for ten years after the divorce. Grover also mentioned Aamir Khan was aware of her marital difficulties.

title: 3 Of Hyderabad Family Die In Road Accident In US
content: Three members of a family from Hyderabad died and two others were injured in a road accident in the US state of California, according to information received by their family here on Tuesday.

title: Trump Vows To Help Lebanon Pursue Peace With Israel
content: US President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought Washington's backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel.

title: Ukraine's Zelensky Sacks Army Chief Amid War
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Tuesday that he was dismissing Oleksandr Syrsky, commander-in-chief of Ukraine's army, and appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place.

title: Pete Hegseth Estimates Iran War Has Cost $37.5 Billion So Far
content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Iran war has cost the United States $37.5 billion so far, up from a previous estimate of nearly $29 billion in mid-May.

title: Weather Office Predicts Heavy Rain, Widespread Thundershowers Across UP
content: Rainfall was recorded in different parts of Uttar Pradesh during the 24 hours till Tuesday evening, the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) said, predicting widespread thundershowers across the state over next few days.

title: 21 Killed In 24 Hours As Flood Situation Worsens In Assam
content: The flood situation in Assam further deteriorated on Tuesday as 21 more persons died, while 5.64 lakh people were affected across 16 districts, an official bulletin said.

title: "India Is Now A Solar Superpower Backed By Skilled Workforce": UN Climate Chief
content: Climate action is proving to be a game-changer for the Indian economy, Simon Stiell, Executive Secretary of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), told reporters in Delhi

title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: Trump Says US 'Not Finished At All' With Iran
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Rahul, Priyanka Gandhi Moved From Protest Outside PM Modi's House
content: Congress workers, led by Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, marched to PM Modi's residence to protest the paper leak issue on Tuesday.

title: Iran Shifted Nuclear Centrifuges To Pickaxe Mountain: Report
content: According to a Wall Street Journal report citing US and Israeli officials, Israeli intelligence believes the transfer took place last autumn, months after the 12-day conflict in June 2025, when US and Israeli forces struck several of Iran's nuclear facilities.

title: "Owe Students More Than Outrage": Dharmendra Pradhan Slams Rahul Gandhi
content: "The students of India deserve far better than being treated as props in a political campaign," Pradhan said.

title: "If Someone Is Hungry, They Eat": Sacked CJP Spokesperson On Burger Video
content: Dahiya told NDTV that the backlash against him was nothing more than "propaganda", insisting that his record of service to the movement spoke for itself.

title: 20 Workers Die In Sikkim Tunnel Collapse, Efforts On To Rescue 5 Trapped
content: At least 20 workers were killed and five others remained trapped for over 24 hours after a tunnel under construction collapsed in Sikkim's Namchi district following an explosion of suspected methane gas, a police officer said on Tuesday.

title: "Why Go To Protest When I Could Study, Improve My Score": NEET Topper To NDTV
content: Panshul Bansal, who secured All India Rank 2 with a 99.9999 percentile in the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination, told NDTV that the cancellation of the original examination initially left him disappointed.

title: Cleric Arrested For Sheltering, Aiding Pakistani Terrorists In J&K
content: In a major crackdown on the terror ecosystem in Jammu's hinterland, Jammu and Kashmir Police have arrested the cleric of a local mosque for providing safe shelter and logistical support to Pakistani terrorists operating in the Chatroo area of Kishtwar.

title: Rahul Gandhi Went Back On His Word After His Demand Was Accepted: Minister
content: Singh said the Centre agreed to the Congress' demand for discussions in Parliament, but another clause was added by Gandhi.

title: "We're Not Finished": Trump Warns Of Strike On Iran's Pickaxe Mountain Nuclear Site
content: Pickaxe Mountain is located near Natanz and is believed by Western intelligence agencies to house an undeclared nuclear enrichment facility buried deep beneath the ground.

title: Karnataka Man Kills Wife, 2 Daughters, Then Dies By Suicide
content: A man allegedly killed his wife and two daughters before dying by suicide in Karnataka's Mysuru district, police said.

title: 'Birthday Celebration' To Sit-In: How Congress Protest Played Out
content: Sources said that when Congress MPs were told to head to Mallikarjun Kharge's house, they were under the impression that they were being invited to celebrate his birthday.

title: 20-Year-Old Woman In ICU After Being Injured At CJP Protests In Delhi
content: The woman, who remains on ventilator support, collided with a barricade during police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar.

title: Pakistan intruder detained along international border in J&K’s Akhnoor
content: the 53-year-old allegedly crossed over the international border in the Akhnoor sector . he allegedly sneaked into Indian territory late Monday . the suspect, who was taken to Malabela BOP for questioning, identified himself as Mohammad Rafiq alias Kala from Pindi village in Pakistan .

title: Assam flood toll rises: 21 killed in a day; over 5.6 lakh affected across 16 districts

title: Omar Abdullah ‘flees’ Jantar Mantar in auto, says CJP supporters followed him
content: Omar Abdullah travelled in the autorickshaw along with his two sons, Zahir and Zamir . he said his now-viral ride was a sudden decision prompted by concerns . supporters of the Cockroach Janata Party started following him .

title: Pilgrimages to shrines in J&K stay suspended for 3rd day amid hostile weather
content: Pilgrimages to the cave shrines of Shri Amarnath, Mata Vaishnodevi, Shiv Khori, and Sarthal Devi in jammu & Kashmir remained suspended for the third consecutive day on Tuesday . schools have been closed till July 22, officials said . heavy rains lashed parts of Udhampur district on Tuesday, triggering mudslides at multiple locations along the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway .

title: New US visa rule gives OPT students temporary extension filing exemption
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': 2 protestors injured; 21-year-old on ventilator, another had pellet in eye
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

title: No legal aid to accused, SC orders fresh trial in 30-year-old blast case
content: supreme court sets aside conviction and death sentence of an accused in 1996 samleti bus blast case . the accused, Abdul Hameed, was represented by a lawyer . it concluded that the accused remained undefended in court proceedings .

title: Sydney mass shooting: Bondi's Indian hero gets Australian residency

title: Modi govt picks a Sikh again to head minorities panel
content: Harjit Singh Grewal is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt . the post had been vacant since April 2025, when lalpura's three-year term ended . mr Grewal's appointment gains significance as it comes ahead of 2027 Punjab assembly polls .

title: Sikkim tunnel collapse: 20 bodies recovered from site, state govt order SIT probe
content: at least 20 of the 27 people trapped in a collapsed tunnel have died . two workers have exited the tunnel and search is on for the rest . nine of the dead are from Jalpaiguri district and two from Alipurduar district .
